title: PyPI name confirmation and v0.1.0 publish
short_description: Confirm the anima-ai PyPI name and anima import name (logging the fallback decision if needed) and publish v0.1.0 — an external action under Arnav's name, flagged to him first per the persona contract.
long_description_md:
# PyPI name confirmation and v0.1.0 publish

## Why this task exists

Launch bar per `docs/cofounder/ROADMAP.md` Phase 8, first done-when bullet: "PyPI name confirmed (`anima-ai`; import-name fallback decision logged if needed) and v0.1.0 published (external action — flagged to Arnav first)." `technical-architecture.md` names the distribution as PyPI package `anima-ai`, import `anima`, explicitly flagging "name availability on PyPI to be confirmed before first publish; fallback import name `animaai`." `repo-scaffold-ci` already set `pyproject.toml` name to `anima-ai` / import `anima` on day one, but that was a design-time pick, not a confirmed-available-at-publish-time fact — names get squatted between Phase 0 and Phase 8. This task closes that gap right before the actual publish, the one moment it matters.

## Roadmap fit

Last phase, first bullet. Every other Phase 8 task (`docs-site-context7`, `launch-assets-posts`) assumes the package is live on PyPI under a confirmed name — `launch-assets-posts` depends on this task directly for that reason. This task depends on `v1-flagship-episode` because Arnav does not authorize a publish until the v1 bar (coherent 15-20 min episode, verify green, founder style checkpoint passed) is actually met — publishing early would ship an unfinished promise.

## Scope

1. Re-check PyPI for squatting on `anima-ai` (project name) and `anima` (import name / any colliding package on PyPI's simple index) right before publish. If either is taken, apply the already-documented fallback (`animaai` import name per `technical-architecture.md`) and update `pyproject.toml` accordingly.
2. Log the confirm-or-fallback decision in `docs/cofounder/DECISIONS.md` either way — this is required even if nothing changed, because it's the record that the check actually happened at publish time, not just at scaffold time.
3. Bump `pyproject.toml` version from its dev placeholder to `0.1.0`, write the `CHANGELOG.md` entry for the release, and tag the release commit locally (`git tag v0.1.0`) — all local, reversible, no external action yet.
4. Publish the built distribution to PyPI under Arnav's account — external, irreversible, under his name. Flag to Arnav first per `COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md`'s narrow exception (spending, publishing externally, third-party signups). Log the publish in `docs/cofounder/DECISIONS.md`.
5. Post-publish smoke test: in a clean virtual environment, `pip install anima-ai` from the real PyPI index and confirm `import anima; anima.__version__ == "0.1.0"` — proves the published artifact is actually installable and importable, not just that `twine upload` returned 0.

## Explicitly out of scope

- Docs site, GitHub Pages, Context7 listing — `docs-site-context7`.
- Launch video assets, Show HN / r/Python posts, issue template, post-launch triage — `launch-assets-posts`.
- Any renderer, verifier, CLI, or style-pack work — all prior phases, frozen by the time this task runs.
- GitHub Actions workflow changes beyond what's needed to build the release artifact locally; this task does not stand up a CI-triggered release pipeline if one doesn't already exist — it publishes the artifact that already passes CI on the tagged commit.

deliverables.description: Re-check PyPI at publish time for squatting on project name `anima-ai` and import name `anima`; if either is taken, apply the documented fallback import name `animaai` and update `pyproject.toml`. Log the confirm-or-fallback decision in `docs/cofounder/DECISIONS.md` regardless of outcome.

verification.steps: 1. Fetch https://pypi.org/pypi/anima-ai/json and https://pypi.org/pypi/anima/json (or the fallback https://pypi.org/pypi/animaai/json) to check current availability/ownership. 2. Confirm `pyproject.toml` name/import fields match the decision (fallback applied if needed). 3. Confirm a new dated entry exists at the end of docs/cofounder/DECISIONS.md documenting the name-confirmation outcome (grep -A5 'PyPI name' docs/cofounder/DECISIONS.md should show the entry).

deliverables.description: Prepare the local release: bump pyproject.toml version to 0.1.0, add the v0.1.0 CHANGELOG.md entry, and tag the release commit v0.1.0 locally — all reversible, no external publish yet.

verification.command: grep -q 'version = "0.1.0"' pyproject.toml && grep -q '0.1.0' CHANGELOG.md && git tag --list v0.1.0 | grep -q v0.1.0

deliverables.description: Publish v0.1.0 to PyPI under Arnav's account (external, irreversible action under his name — flag to him first per COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md, log the publish in docs/cofounder/DECISIONS.md), then smoke-test the published artifact from a clean virtual environment.

verification.steps: 1. Flag to Arnav first per COFOUNDER_PERSONA.md's narrow exception before doing anything external/irreversible: this is a real PyPI publish under his name. 2. Build the distribution (python -m build) and upload with twine upload dist/anima_ai-0.1.0* using Arnav's PyPI credentials, only after his go-ahead. 3. Log the publish (package, version, timestamp, PyPI URL) in docs/cofounder/DECISIONS.md. 4. In a fresh venv run: python -m venv /tmp/anima-smoke && /tmp/anima-smoke/bin/pip install anima-ai==0.1.0 && /tmp/anima-smoke/bin/python -c "import anima; assert anima.__version__ == '0.1.0'; print('OK')" and confirm it prints OK, proving the published artifact installs and imports correctly from the real index.
